narrative:
The Karnataka High Court recorded a 47.9% overall hearing rate on June 16, 2026, resolving 1,321 out of 2,760 scheduled cases across 47 judges. This activity marks a minor decline from the 48.9% hearing rate achieved on the previous day (June 15), but demonstrates a substantial performance improvement compared to the prior week's benchmarks on June 12 (40.1%) and June 11 (40.2%).

Among individual judges, Justice S. Vishwajith Shetty in Hall 19 managed the heaviest workload, hearing 109 out of 274 scheduled cases (39.8%). Justice M. Nagaprasanna in Hall 14 also handled significant volumes, successfully converting 74 of 147 scheduled cases (50.3%) and 51 of 75 cases (68.0%) across separate lists. Additionally, the bench of the Chief Justice & K.S. Hemalekha maintained high efficiency, converting 73.0% of their primary 63-case list and 80.6% of a secondary 31-case list. 

In terms of procedural stages, Orders constituted the largest category with 924 scheduled cases and a conversion rate of 53.6%, representing a moderate drop from yesterday's 56.8% rate. Preliminary Hearings and Admissions both stood at a 44.4% hearing rate, while Final Hearings lagged behind at 32.5%. Writ Petitions (WP) remained the dominant case type, comprising 1,162 of the scheduled listings and achieving a 44.7% hearing rate, which is a slight increase from yesterday's 43.8%. Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) and Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) followed with 49.0% and 48.1% clearance rates respectively, while Criminal Revision Petitions (CRL.RP) registered a notable 83.8% conversion rate.
